faqItems.question: How does the per-module pricing work?
faqItems.answer: You only pay for the modules you use. Each module has its own pricing based on the number of employees in your company. You can start with one module and add more as your needs grow.

faqItems.question: Is there a minimum contract period?
faqItems.answer: No. All our plans are month-to-month with no long-term commitment required. You can cancel or change modules at any time.

faqItems.question: Can I try before I buy?
faqItems.answer: Yes! We offer a 60-day free trial for all modules. No credit card required. Start using the platform immediately and see the value for yourself.

faqItems.question: What happens to my data if I cancel?
faqItems.answer: Your data remains accessible for 30 days after cancellation. You can export all your data at any time. We follow GDPR best practices for data retention and deletion.

faqItems.question: Do you offer discounts for multiple modules?
faqItems.answer: Yes, we offer bundle discounts when you combine multiple modules. The more modules you add, the better the per-module price. Contact us for a personalized quote.

list.name: Employment Contract Template
list.description: A comprehensive employment contract template compliant with the Bulgarian Labour Code, covering all essential clauses from probation periods to termination conditions.
whatsIncluded: Probation period clauses (up to 6 months per Art. 70 Labour Code)
Working hours, overtime, and rest period provisions
howToUse: Download and review the complete template
Fill in employer and employee details in the designated fields
Customize clauses to match your company policies
Review with legal counsel for compliance with current Bulgarian labour legislation
Print two copies — one for employer, one for employee
list.seoContent: Under the Bulgarian Labour Code (Kodeks na truda), every employment relationship must be formalized with a written contract before the employee starts working. The contract must be registered with the National Revenue Agency (NRA) within 3 days. Key mandatory elements include job title, workplace location, duration (fixed-term or indefinite), working hours, base salary, and annual paid leave entitlement (minimum 20 working days per Art. 155). Probation periods can be up to 6 months and must be explicitly stated. This template covers all requirements of Articles 62-70 of the Bulgarian Labour Code.

list.name: Job Description Template
list.description: Structured job description template with sections for responsibilities, requirements, benefits and salary ranges — optimized for the Bulgarian job market.
whatsIncluded: Salary range guidance for Bulgarian market
howToUse: Start by defining the role's primary purpose in one sentence
List 5-8 core responsibilities in order of importance
Separate must-have requirements from nice-to-have qualifications
Include salary range (candidates in Bulgaria expect transparency)
Add your company culture section and benefits
Have the hiring manager review before publishing
list.seoContent: A well-crafted job description is essential for attracting quality candidates in the Bulgarian labour market. According to research, job postings with salary ranges receive 30% more applications. Bulgarian job seekers value transparency about working conditions, including whether the role is on-site, hybrid, or remote. The National Classification of Professions and Positions (NKPD) should be referenced for correct job code classification, which is required for labour contract registration with the NRA. Include information about social benefits, as these are a key differentiator in the competitive Bulgarian tech and business market.

list.name: Leave Policy Template
list.description: Complete leave policy template covering all types of leave under Bulgarian law — annual, sick, maternity, paternity, and special leave provisions.
whatsIncluded: Annual paid leave (minimum 20 days per Art. 155)
Sick leave procedures and documentation requirements
Unpaid leave provisions and approval process
Public holidays and bridge day policies
howToUse: Review the legal minimums outlined in the template
Decide on any additional leave days above the legal minimum
Customize the approval workflow to match your organization
Set clear procedures for sick leave documentation
Define the process for requesting and tracking leave
Distribute to all employees and update your employee handbook
list.seoContent: Bulgarian labour law provides some of the most generous leave entitlements in the EU. Under Art. 155 of the Labour Code, every employee is entitled to a minimum of 20 working days of paid annual leave. Employees under 18 get at least 26 days. Workers in hazardous conditions receive additional leave. Sick leave is covered by the employer for the first 3 days (70% of salary per Art. 40(5) of the Social Security Code) and by the National Social Security Institute (NOI) from day 4 onwards (80-90% of salary). Maternity leave totals 410 calendar days — 45 days before the due date and 365 days after birth, paid at 90% of the average gross salary. Fathers are entitled to 15 days of paternity leave after the child's birth.

list.name: Onboarding Checklist
list.description: Structured 90-day onboarding checklist covering pre-boarding, first day, first week, and first three months — ensuring new hires are set up for success.
whatsIncluded: First week goals and team introductions
howToUse: Share the pre-boarding section with IT and admin teams one week before start date
Assign a buddy or mentor from the new hire's team
Walk through the Day 1 checklist with the new employee
Schedule all milestone check-ins in advance
Use the 30/60/90 templates for structured feedback conversations
Complete the probation review before the end of the trial period
list.seoContent: Effective onboarding is critical for employee retention — research shows that structured onboarding improves new hire retention by 82%. In Bulgaria, probation periods can last up to 6 months (Art. 70 of the Labour Code), making the first 90 days especially important. During probation, either party can terminate the contract with no notice and no reason given. A good onboarding process should cover legal requirements (signing the labour contract, NRA registration, workplace safety briefing per the Healthy and Safe Working Conditions Act), practical setup (equipment, accounts, access), and cultural integration (team introductions, company values, communication norms).

list.name: Performance Review Form
list.description: 360-degree performance review template with self-assessment, manager evaluation, peer feedback sections and goal-setting framework.
whatsIncluded: Manager evaluation form with rating scales
Development plan and training needs assessment
Overall performance summary and next steps
howToUse: Distribute self-assessment forms 2 weeks before the review meeting
Collect peer feedback from 3-5 colleagues who work closely with the employee
Manager completes their evaluation independently
Schedule a 60-minute review meeting in a private setting
Discuss results openly and agree on goals for the next period
Document action items and follow up within 30 days
list.seoContent: Regular performance reviews are essential for talent retention and development in Bulgaria's competitive job market. While Bulgarian labour law does not mandate formal performance reviews, they are critical for documenting employee performance — especially relevant during probation periods (Art. 71 of the Labour Code) and when considering promotions or terminations. Best practices for Bulgarian companies include conducting reviews at least twice per year, aligning individual goals with company objectives, and documenting all assessments in writing. The 360-degree feedback approach, combining self-assessment with manager and peer input, provides the most comprehensive view of employee performance.

list.name: Termination Document Template
list.description: Legally compliant termination notice and separation agreement template covering all grounds for termination under the Bulgarian Labour Code.
howToUse: Determine the legal basis for termination (with/without notice, mutual, disciplinary)
Calculate the required notice period (minimum 30 days, or as stated in contract)
Compute severance obligations (if applicable)
Prepare the termination document using the appropriate template section
Have the document reviewed by legal counsel
Deliver the notice in writing and obtain signed acknowledgment
list.seoContent: Termination of employment in Bulgaria is strictly regulated by the Labour Code (Articles 325-335). Employers must follow specific procedures depending on the type of termination. Mutual termination (Art. 325, para. 1) requires written agreement from both parties. Employer-initiated termination with notice (Art. 328) requires at least 30 days' notice (or up to 3 months if specified in the contract) and must cite one of the legally recognized grounds. Termination without notice (Art. 330) is limited to serious breaches. Severance pay is mandatory in certain cases: employees terminated due to company closure or downsizing are entitled to compensation equal to at least 1 month's gross salary. Employees with 10+ years of service at the same employer who reach retirement age receive 2 months' salary (6 months if with 10+ years of service in the same company per Art. 222(3)).

employmentCostCalculator.metaTitle: Employment Cost Calculator Bulgaria 2026 | Total Employer Cost
employmentCostCalculator.metaDescription: Calculate the total cost of employing staff in Bulgaria for 2026. See employer social security contributions, health insurance, and team cost projections.
employmentCostCalculator.heading: Employment Cost Calculator Bulgaria 2026
employmentCostCalculator.introP1: Calculate the true cost of hiring employees in Bulgaria. Beyond gross salary, employers must pay social security, health insurance, and pension contributions that add approximately 18-19% to the base salary cost.
employmentCostCalculator.introP2: Use this calculator to project monthly and annual costs for your entire team, helping you budget accurately for your Bulgarian workforce.
employmentCostCalculator.bornAfter1960: Employees born after 31 Dec 1959 (universal pension fund applies)
employmentCostCalculator.disclaimer: Calculations use 2026 Bulgarian social security rates for Category III workers. Actual costs may vary based on industry risk classification and collective agreements.
employmentCostCalculator.obligationsTitle: Employer Social Security Obligations in Bulgaria
employmentCostCalculator.obligationsP1: Bulgarian employers are required by law to register employees with the National Revenue Agency (NRA) and the National Social Security Institute (NSSI). Social security contributions must be paid monthly by the 25th of the following month.
employmentCostCalculator.obligationsP2: The employer bears the larger share of contributions (approximately 60% of the total). For Category III workers (the most common classification for office and IT professionals), the total employer contribution rate is approximately 18.92% of insurable income for employees born after 1960.
employmentCostCalculator.penaltiesP1: Failure to register employees or pay social security contributions on time results in significant penalties. The NRA may impose fines of €767 to €7,669 for first offenses, and €1,534 to €10,226 for repeated violations. Additionally, late payments accrue interest at the statutory rate.
employmentCostCalculator.optimizationP1: While social security rates are fixed by law, companies can optimize total employment costs through meal vouchers (tax-exempt up to €102/month), voluntary health insurance, and professional development budgets. These benefits are either tax-deductible or exempt from social security contributions, making them cost-effective alternatives to gross salary increases.
employmentCostCalculator.ctaDesc: Let HR calculate labour costs and track social security contributions automatically.

leaveCalculator.metaTitle: Leave Calculator Bulgaria 2026 | Annual Leave Entitlement
leaveCalculator.metaDescription: Calculate leave balances and entitlements under Bulgarian labour law for 2026.
leaveCalculator.heading: Leave Calculator Bulgaria 2026
leaveCalculator.introP1: Calculate your employees' annual leave entitlement under the Bulgarian Labour Code. The minimum statutory leave in Bulgaria is 20 working days, with additional days for seniority, disability or hazardous work.
leaveCalculator.introP2: Use this calculator to determine the exact leave entitlement for each employee based on their circumstances.
leaveCalculator.disclaimer: Calculations based on 2026 Bulgarian Labour Code provisions. Individual collective agreements may grant additional leave.
leaveCalculator.laborCodeTitle: Leave Entitlement Under Bulgarian Labour Code
leaveCalculator.laborCodeP1: Under Article 155 of the Bulgarian Labour Code, all employees are entitled to a minimum of 20 working days paid annual leave. This minimum cannot be reduced by employment contract or collective agreement.
leaveCalculator.laborCodeP2: Additional leave is granted for: employees under 18 (26 days minimum), employees with disabilities (26 days minimum), and employees in hazardous or night-shift work (Art. 156).
leaveCalculator.maternityP1: Bulgarian law provides 410 days of maternity leave (Art. 163 Labour Code), of which 45 days must be taken before birth. Parental leave is also available until the child reaches 2 years of age.
leaveCalculator.sickLeaveP1: The employer pays the first day of sick leave (Art. 40 Social Insurance Code). From the second day onwards, the National Social Security Institute (NSSI) pays 80% of the average daily wage for the last 18 months.
leaveCalculator.ctaDesc: Let HR handle leave requests, approvals and balance tracking automatically.
leaveCalculator.yearsOfServiceHint: Additional leave is granted after 5, 10 and 15 years of service

salaryCalculator.metaTitle: Salary Calculator Bulgaria 2026 | Net & Gross Salary
salaryCalculator.metaDescription: Calculate net and gross salary in Bulgaria with social security and income tax for 2026.
salaryCalculator.heading: Salary Calculator Bulgaria 2026
salaryCalculator.introP1: Calculate exact net take-home pay or gross salary for Bulgarian employees. Includes all 2026 social security and health insurance rates.
salaryCalculator.introP2: Switch between Gross-to-Net and Net-to-Gross calculations.
salaryCalculator.howItWorksTitle: How salary calculations work in Bulgaria
salaryCalculator.howItWorksP1: Bulgarian social security contributions are split between employee and employer. The employee pays approximately 13.78% and the employer pays approximately 18.92% on top of the gross salary.
salaryCalculator.howItWorksP2: Income tax is flat 10% applied to taxable income (gross minus employee social security contributions).
salaryCalculator.socialSecurityP1: The total social security rate for Category III workers is 32.7% — split between employee (13.78%) and employer (18.92%).
salaryCalculator.socialSecurityP2: Contributions are calculated on insurable income, which must be between the minimum and maximum insurable income set annually by the National Assembly.
salaryCalculator.employerCostsP1: The true cost of an employee is their gross salary plus all employer social security contributions. This is typically 18-19% above the gross salary.
salaryCalculator.ctaDesc: Let HR handle salary calculations, payslips and compliance automatically.

freelancerComparison.metaTitle: Freelancer vs Employment Calculator Bulgaria 2026 | EOOD vs Payroll
freelancerComparison.metaDescription: Compare freelancer EOOD income with employment salary in Bulgaria for 2026. See the real tax difference, hidden costs, and net take-home pay side by side.
freelancerComparison.heading: Freelancer vs Employment Calculator Bulgaria 2026
freelancerComparison.introP1: Should you work as a freelancer through an EOOD or accept a payroll position? This calculator compares net take-home pay, effective tax rates, and hidden costs for both options based on the same gross monthly amount.
freelancerComparison.introP2: Enter your expected monthly revenue and see a complete side-by-side breakdown of taxes, social security contributions, overhead costs, and benefits for EOOD ownership versus standard employment in Bulgaria.

input.monthlyAmountHelp: Enter the gross monthly amount before any taxes or deductions
input.bornAfter1960: Born after 31 Dec 1959 (universal pension fund applies)
input.illnessMaternityNote: Self-insured persons can opt in for illness and maternity coverage at an additional 3.5% contribution
input.vatNote: VAT registration is mandatory when annual turnover exceeds €51,130. If registered, you charge 20% VAT on invoices.

eood.title: EOOD (Freelancer)
eood.vatNote: VAT is collected and remitted to the state — it does not affect your net income

benefits.title: Benefits Comparison
benefits.sickPayEood: Only if opted in for illness insurance
benefits.sickPayEmployment: Employer pays first day, then 80% from NSSI
benefits.unemploymentEmployment: Up to 12 months based on service
benefits.mortgageEood: Harder — banks require 2+ years of accounts

freelancerComparison.disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates based on 2026 Bulgarian tax rates and social security contributions. Actual results may vary based on individual circumstances, industry classification, and specific EOOD expenses. Consult a licensed accountant for personalized advice.

cta.title: Ready to Optimize Your Compensation?
cta.description: Whether you choose freelancing or employment, HR helps you manage payroll, taxes, and compliance automatically.

seo.howItWorksTitle: How the Freelancer vs Employment Comparison Works
seo.howItWorksP1: This calculator models two scenarios: running a single-person EOOD (the most common freelancer structure in Bulgaria) and standard employment under a labour contract. For the EOOD scenario, it accounts for self-insurance on the minimum base (€551 for 2026), corporate tax at 10%, dividend tax at 5%, and typical monthly overhead including accountant fees, bank charges, and administrative time.
seo.howItWorksP2: For the employment scenario, it calculates the standard employee social security deductions (13.78% for those born after 1960), flat income tax at 10%, and shows the total employer cost including the employer's share of contributions (18.92%). The comparison also factors in the monetary value of employment benefits like paid leave, sick pay, and labour law protection.
seo.eoodVsEmploymentTitle: EOOD vs Employment in Bulgaria: Key Tax Differences
seo.eoodVsEmploymentP1: The main tax advantage of an EOOD is that self-insurance contributions are calculated on a chosen base (minimum €551), not on actual revenue. This means high earners pay proportionally less in social security. However, EOOD income is taxed twice: first as corporate profit (10%), then as dividends (5%), resulting in an effective combined rate of 14.5% on distributed profits.
seo.eoodVsEmploymentP2: Employment offers a simpler tax structure but higher effective rates for above-average earners. The total social security burden is 32.7% of insurable income (split between employee and employer), plus 10% income tax. However, employees gain significant non-monetary benefits: 20+ days of paid leave, full sick pay coverage, maternity benefits based on actual salary, unemployment insurance, and full protection under the Bulgarian Labour Code.
seo.hiddenCostsTitle: Hidden Costs of Running an EOOD in Bulgaria
seo.hiddenCostsP1: Beyond taxes, EOOD owners face ongoing costs that reduce the apparent tax advantage: mandatory accounting services (€51–153/month), bank maintenance fees (€8–15/month), annual financial statement filing, and the time cost of administrative tasks like invoicing, tax declarations, and dealing with the NRA. Registration costs (approximately €256–409) and potential deregistration costs should also be amortized. These overhead costs can total €102–256 monthly, significantly narrowing the gap between freelancer and employment net income, especially for revenues below €2,556.

about.metaDescription: Learn more about HR — the Bulgarian HR platform for small and medium businesses. Meet our team, mission and values.

hero.heading: About HR
hero.subheading: We're building the HR platform Bulgarian companies truly need — simple, compliant, and powerful.

story.heading: Our Story
story.content1: HR was founded in Sofia by a team of HR professionals and engineers who saw firsthand how Bulgarian SMEs struggle with outdated HR processes, manual spreadsheets and expensive enterprise software that doesn't fit their needs.
story.content2: We built a platform combining powerful automation with a simple, intuitive interface — so HR teams can focus on people, not paperwork. Today hundreds of Bulgarian companies trust HR to manage their entire employee lifecycle.

mission.heading: Our Mission & Values
mission.content: To make quality HR management accessible to every Bulgarian company, regardless of size or budget. We believe every employee deserves a professional onboarding, every candidate deserves a respectful process, and every HR team deserves tools that actually work.

simplicity.title: Simplicity
simplicity.description: HR software shouldn't require a PhD to use. Every feature prioritizes ease of use and fast value.

items.answer1: Sign up for free. No credit card required. Start posting jobs and managing candidates immediately.

cookies.metaDescription: Learn how HR uses cookies and similar technologies to provide you with a better experience.

hero.heading: Cookie Policy
hero.subheading: How HR uses cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience.

whatAre.title: What Are Cookies
whatAre.description: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit our website. They help us provide a better experience by remembering your preferences and understanding how you use our platform.
whatAre.purpose: Cookies serve various purposes — from keeping you logged in to helping us understand which features are most useful.

types.title: Types of Cookies We Use
types.description: We use the following categories of cookies on our platform:

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: Required for the platform to function. These cannot be disabled.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.description: Remember your preferences and settings to personalise your experience.
functional.examples: Language preference, UI theme, notification settings

performance.title: Performance Cookies
performance.description: Help us understand how visitors use our site so we can improve it.
performance.examples: Page views, session duration, error tracking

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: Used to deliver relevant advertising and track campaign effectiveness.

cookieTable.title: Cookie Details
cookieTable.description: Specific cookies used on our platform:

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.description: We use services from these third-party providers that may set cookies:

analytics.description: We use Google Analytics to understand how visitors interact with our site. Google may set cookies to track usage.

linkedin.description: We use LinkedIn Insight Tag to measure the effectiveness of our LinkedIn advertising campaigns.

marketing.description: We use marketing tools that may set cookies to help us reach potential customers across the web.

managing.title: Managing Your Cookie Preferences
managing.description: You have control over the cookies we use. Here's how to manage them:

consent.title: Cookie Consent Banner
consent.description: When you first visit our site, you can accept or decline non-essential cookies via our consent banner. You can update your preferences at any time.

browser.title: Browser Settings
browser.description: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies.
browser.popular: Popular browser guides: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge

impact.title: Impact of Disabling Cookies
impact.description: Disabling essential cookies will prevent you from logging in and using core platform features. Disabling functional cookies may affect your personalised experience.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. We'll notify you of significant changes by posting a notice on our website or by email. The date at the top of this page indicates when it was last revised.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact us:
contact.addressDetails: Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria

systemStatus.metaDescription: Check the real-time status of all HR services.

hero.heading: System Status
hero.subheading: Real-time status of all HR services and infrastructure.

services.title: Service Status
services.description: Current status of all platform services.

search.title: Search
search.description: Full-text search for job postings, candidates, and documents

fileStorage.title: File Storage
fileStorage.description: Document storage, CV uploads, and file management

incidents.title: Active Incidents
incidents.description: Any current incidents will be listed here.

incident1.title: Increased API Latency
incident1.description: Increased API response times due to database load. The issue was identified and resolved.

incident2.title: Email Delivery Delays
incident2.description: Email notifications were delayed by up to 30 minutes due to a third-party provider issue.

incident3.title: Scheduled Maintenance
incident3.description: Planned database maintenance window. Read-only mode was active during maintenance.

subscribe.title: Subscribe to notifications
subscribe.description: Receive alerts for incidents and maintenance windows.

partners.metaDescription: Join the HR partner program and grow your business with us.

hero.heading: Become a Partner
hero.subheading: Grow your business by becoming a HR partner. Competitive commissions and full partner support.

why.title: Why Become Our Partner?
why.subheading: Join a growing ecosystem and build a stream of recurring revenue.

revenue.title: Recurring Revenue
revenue.description: Earn competitive commissions for every client you refer or resell.

training.title: Partner Training
training.description: Comprehensive onboarding, certification programs, and ongoing product training.

coMarketing.title: Co-Marketing
coMarketing.description: Joint campaigns, co-branded materials, events, and shared lead generation.

support.title: Dedicated Support
support.description: Your own partner manager, priority technical support, and sales resources.

types.title: Partnership Types
types.subheading: Choose the partner model that fits your business.

referral.title: Referral Partner
referral.description: Refer clients and earn a commission for every successful deal.

reseller.title: Reseller Partner
reseller.description: Resell HR under your own brand with custom pricing.

integration.title: Integration Partner
integration.description: Build a native integration with HR and reach our customer base.

technology.title: Technology Partner
technology.description: Deeper technical collaboration for building joint solutions.

current.title: Current Partners
current.subheading: Trusted by leading companies in HR technology.

form.title: Apply to Become a Partner
form.description: Tell us about your business and we’ll get back to you within 2 business days.
form.agreement: I agree to the terms of the partner program
form.successMessage: Thank you for your interest in partnering with HR. Our team will review your application and get back to you within 2 business days.

contact.title: Have Questions?
contact.description: Our partner team is here to help.

privacy.metaDescription: Learn how HR collects, uses, and protects your personal data. Compliant with GDPR and applicable data protection law.

hero.heading: Privacy Policy
hero.subheading: How we collect, use, and protect your personal data in accordance with GDPR and applicable data protection legislation.

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
introduction.content: HR Ltd (“we”, “us”, “our”) is committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and share your personal data when you use the HR platform and related services.
introduction.scope: This policy applies to all users of the HR platform, including employers, HR administrators, and employees. It has been prepared in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (the General Data Protection Regulation / GDPR) and applicable national data protection legislation.

controller.title: 2. Data Controller
controller.description: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:
controller.addressValue: Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria

information.title: 3. Data We Collect
information.description: We collect the following categories of personal data depending on your role and how you use the platform:

personal.title: Identity Data
personal.description: Data you provide directly when creating an account or using the service:

items.name: Full name, job title, and organisational role
items.company: Company name, registration number, and VAT number

usage.title: Usage and Technical Data
usage.description: Data collected automatically when you interact with the platform:

items.device: Device type, operating system, and screen resolution
items.browser: Browser type and version, IP address

employment.title: Employment Data
employment.description: Data processed on behalf of your employer through the HR platform:

items.cv: CVs, cover letters, and application materials
items.leave: Leave balances, requests, and attendance records

usage.title: 4. How We Use Your Data
usage.description: We process your personal data for the following purposes:

items.service: Providing and maintaining the HR platform and its features
items.support: Responding to your enquiries and providing technical support
items.improvement: Analysing usage patterns to improve our services and user experience
items.communication: Sending notifications, updates, and (with your consent) marketing communications
items.security: Detecting and preventing fraud, abuse, and security threats
items.legal: Complying with legal obligations under applicable law, including tax reporting

legalBasis.title: 5. Legal Basis (GDPR Article 6)
legalBasis.description: We process your personal data on the following legal bases as defined in Article 6(1) of the GDPR:

consent.title: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
consent.description: For marketing communications, non-essential cookies, and analytics. You may withdraw your consent at any time via account settings or cookie preferences.

contract.title: Contractual Necessity (Art. 6(1)(b))
contract.description: Processing necessary to perform our contract with you, including account management, service delivery, billing, and customer support.

legal.title: Legal Obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))
legal.description: Processing required to comply with applicable tax law, accounting law, employment law, and social security reporting obligations.

interest.title: Legitimate Interests (Art. 6(1)(f))
interest.description: Processing necessary for our legitimate business interests, including platform security, fraud prevention, service improvement, and direct marketing to existing customers. We conduct a balancing test to ensure your rights are not overridden.

sharing.title: 6. Data Sharing
sharing.description: We are committed to keeping your data confidential. We share personal data only in limited circumstances:

never.title: What We Never Do
never.content: We never sell your personal data to third parties. We never share employee data with other employers or advertisers. We never use your data for purposes unrelated to our service.

exceptions.title: When We May Share Data
exceptions.description: We may share your data in the following limited circumstances:

items.consent: With your explicit consent for specific purposes
items.providers: With trusted service providers (sub-processors) who help operate the platform, under strict data processing agreements
items.legal: When required by applicable law, court order, or regulatory request
items.safety: To protect the safety, rights, or property of our users or the public
items.transfer: In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with advance notice to affected users

international.title: 7. International Transfers
international.description: Your data is stored primarily within the European Union (AWS Frankfurt). Where a transfer of data outside the EU/EEA is necessary for specific services, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place.
international.safeguards: Transfer safeguards include: EU adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, and supplementary measures recommended by the EDPB. We do not transfer data to countries without adequate protection unless such safeguards are in place.

retention.title: 8. Data Retention
retention.description: We retain your personal data only for as long as necessary for the purposes described in this policy:

items.active: Account data: retained for the duration of the subscription plus 30 days for data export
items.legal: Financial records: retained for 5 years as required by accounting law, and 10 years for tax documentation
items.deletion: On account deletion: personal data is anonymised or deleted within 30 days, unless statutory retention periods apply

rights.title: 9. Your Rights
rights.description: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights in relation to your personal data:

access.title: Right of Access (Art. 15)
access.content: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you, including the purposes of processing and categories of recipients.

correction.title: Right to Rectification (Art. 16)
correction.content: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. You can update most data directly through your account settings.

deletion.title: Right to Erasure (Art. 17)
deletion.content: Request deletion of your personal data when it is no longer needed for the original purpose, subject to statutory retention requirements.

portability.title: Right to Data Portability (Art. 20)
portability.content: Receive your personal data in a structured, machine-readable format (JSON/CSV) and transfer it to another service provider.

restriction.title: Right to Restriction (Art. 18)
restriction.content: Request restriction of the processing of your data while a dispute is being resolved or accuracy is being verified.

objection.title: Right to Object (Art. 21)
objection.content: Object to processing based on legitimate interests or direct marketing at any time. We will cease processing unless we can demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds.

rights.exerciseNote: To exercise any of these rights, contact our DPO at dpo@hr.com. We will respond within 30 days (extendable by 60 days for complex requests), as required by the GDPR. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your national data protection supervisory authority.

children.title: 10. Children’s Privacy
children.content: HR is a platform for businesses and is not intended for persons under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected data from a minor, please contact us immediately at privacy@hr.com and we will delete it without delay.

changes.title: 11. Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or other factors. We will notify you of material changes at least 30 days in advance by email and/or in-app notification. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page. We recommend reviewing this policy regularly.

contact.title: 12. Contact the DPO
contact.description: For questions about this Privacy Policy or to exercise your data protection rights, please contact:
contact.addressDetails: Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria
contact.supervisoryDetails: Your national data protection authority (e.g. ICO in the UK, CNIL in France). In Bulgaria: Commission for Personal Data Protection (CPDP), www.cpdp.bg

summary.title: Summary
summary.content: We collect only the data necessary to provide our HR platform services, process it lawfully under the GDPR, and protect it with industry-standard security measures. Your rights to access, rectification, erasure, and data portability are fully supported.
summary.trust: Your trust is the foundation of our business.
summary.commitment: We are committed to transparency and data protection.

terms.metaDescription: Read the Terms of Service for the HR platform. Governed by applicable law.

hero.heading: Terms of Service
hero.subheading: Please read these terms carefully before using the HR platform.

introduction.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
introduction.content: HR Ltd, a company registered under applicable law, with registered office at Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria (“Company”, “we”, “us”, “our”), operates the HR platform and related services. These Terms of Service (“Terms”) constitute a legally binding agreement between you and the Company.
introduction.acceptance: By accessing or using our platform, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you must not use our services. These Terms apply to all visitors, users, and paid subscribers.

services.title: 2. Description of Service
services.description: HR provides a cloud-based human resources management platform (“Service”) which includes, but is not limited to:

items.item1: Applicant Tracking System (ATS) with AI-powered matching
items.item2: Employee database management and self-service portal
items.item3: Leave tracking with automated approvals and balance calculations
items.item4: Payroll processing compliant with applicable regulations

services.scope: The Service is designed for businesses and is compliant with applicable employment legislation and EU regulations. Features may vary depending on your subscription plan.

account.title: 3. User Accounts
account.description: The creation and management of accounts is subject to the following conditions:

registration.title: Registration
registration.content: You must provide accurate, complete, and up-to-date information when creating an account, including the legal company name and registration number where applicable. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials. Each user must have a separate account; shared accounts are not permitted.

termination.title: Account Closure
termination.content: You may close your account at any time via account settings. Upon termination, we will retain your data for the statutory period required by applicable accounting and tax legislation before secure deletion.

responsibilities.title: 4. Acceptable Use
responsibilities.description: As a HR user, you agree to use the platform responsibly and in compliance with all applicable laws:

accuracy.title: Data Accuracy
accuracy.content: You must ensure that all data entered into the platform, including employee records, job postings, and salary information, is accurate and up to date. Inaccurate data may lead to non-compliance with applicable employment regulations.

compliance.title: Legal Compliance
compliance.content: You must comply with all applicable laws and regulations, including employment law and anti-discrimination directives, when using the platform for recruitment and HR management.

security.title: Account Security
security.content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your login credentials and for all activity under your account. You must notify us immediately at security@hr.com of any unauthorised access or security breach.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Activities
prohibited.description: You may not use the Service to:

items.spam: Send unsolicited messages or spam through the platform
items.malware: Upload malware, viruses, or harmful code
items.scraping: Scrape, crawl, or extract data from the platform using automated tools
items.impersonation: Impersonate another person, organisation, or entity
items.illegal: Engage in activities that violate applicable law, including discriminatory hiring practices

intellectual.title: 5. Intellectual Property
intellectual.description: Intellectual property rights are protected under applicable copyright law and international treaties.

ours.title: Our Property
ours.content: The HR platform, including its software, design, logos, documentation, and all related materials, is the exclusive intellectual property of HR Ltd. You receive a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable licence to use the Service during your subscription period. You may not copy, modify, decompile, or distribute any part of the platform.

yours.title: Your Content
yours.content: All data and content you upload to the platform (employee records, documents, job postings) remains your property. By uploading content, you grant us a limited licence to process, store, and display it solely for the purpose of providing the Service. This licence terminates upon deletion of the content or closure of your account.

fees.title: 6. Payment Terms
fees.description: Paid subscriptions are governed by the following terms, in accordance with applicable consumer protection and e-commerce legislation:

subscription.title: Subscription Plans
subscription.content: Paid plans are billed monthly or annually in advance. Prices are shown on our Pricing page and may be quoted in your local currency or EUR. Applicable taxes (e.g. VAT) will be added where required by law.

refunds.title: Refunds and Cancellation
refunds.content: We offer a 14-day free trial for new accounts. After the trial period, payments are non-refundable except where required by applicable consumer protection law. You may cancel your subscription at any time; the service will continue until the end of the current billing period.

taxes.title: Taxes and Invoicing
taxes.content: All invoices comply with applicable VAT requirements. Electronic invoices are issued automatically upon payment and are available in your account. Business customers must provide a valid company registration number and VAT number for correct invoicing.

privacy.title: 7. Data Processing
privacy.description: We take data protection seriously. As a data processor under the GDPR, we process personal data only to the extent necessary to provide the Service and in accordance with our Data Processing Agreement.
privacy.policyLink2: and GDPR Compliance page for full details on how your data is collected, processed, and protected.

liability.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
liability.description: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:

items.item1: We are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, including loss of profit or data
items.item2: Our total cumulative liability shall not exceed the amounts paid by you in the 12 months preceding the claim
items.item3: We are not liable for the acts or omissions of third-party service providers integrated with the platform
items.item4: We are not liable for service interruptions caused by events beyond our reasonable control (force majeure)

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.content: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless HR Ltd, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising from your use of the platform, breach of these Terms, or infringement of any third-party rights.

termination.title: 9. Termination
termination.description: Either party may terminate this agreement under the following conditions:

byUser.title: Termination by You
byUser.content: You may terminate your account at any time via account settings. After termination, you may export your data within 30 days. After this period, data will be securely deleted unless statutory retention is required.

byUs.title: Termination by Us
byUs.content: We may suspend or terminate your access immediately if you materially breach these Terms, fail to pay subscription fees after 30 days, use the platform for illegal activities, or pose a security risk to other users. We will provide reasonable notice except in cases of serious breach.

governing.title: 10. Governing Law
governing.content: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which HR Ltd is registered, as well as applicable EU regulations including Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).
governing.disputes: Any disputes arising from or related to these Terms shall be resolved by the competent courts in the jurisdiction of our registered office, unless mandatory consumer protection rules require otherwise. Before commencing legal proceedings, the parties shall attempt to resolve disputes amicably through direct negotiation for a period of at least 30 days.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these Terms from time to time to reflect changes in our services, legal requirements, or business practices. We will notify you at least 30 days before material changes take effect by email and in-app notification. Continued use of the Service after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

contact.title: 11. Contact Information
contact.description: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us:
contact.addressDetails: Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria

summary.title: Summary
summary.content: These Terms govern your use of the HR platform. They are designed to protect both parties fairly and transparently.
summary.agreement: If you do not agree with these Terms, please do not use HR.
summary.acceptance: By creating an account, you accept these Terms in full.

gdpr.metaDescription: Learn about HR’s commitment to GDPR compliance, data protection measures, and your rights as a data subject.

hero.heading: GDPR Compliance
hero.subheading: Our commitment to protecting your personal data under European and applicable national data protection legislation.

overview.title: 1. Our Commitment
overview.description: HR Ltd is fully committed to compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (the General Data Protection Regulation / GDPR) and applicable national data protection legislation. As a data controller and processor, we implement comprehensive measures to protect personal data throughout its lifecycle.

keyPrinciples.title: Core GDPR Principles We Follow (Article 5)
keyPrinciples.lawfulness: Lawfulness — We process data only with a valid legal basis (Article 6)
keyPrinciples.fairness: Fairness — We process data in a way that data subjects would reasonably expect
keyPrinciples.transparency: Transparency — We clearly communicate what data we collect and why
keyPrinciples.purpose: Purpose Limitation — We collect data only for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes
keyPrinciples.minimization: Data Minimisation — We collect only the data necessary for each purpose
keyPrinciples.accuracy: Accuracy — We keep personal data accurate and up to date
keyPrinciples.limitation: Storage Limitation — We retain data only for as long as necessary
keyPrinciples.integrity: Integrity and Confidentiality — We protect data with appropriate technical and organisational measures

dpa.title: 2. Data Processing Agreement
dpa.description: When HR processes personal data on behalf of our customers (as a data processor under Article 28 GDPR), we enter into a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) that establishes clear obligations and safeguards:

scope.title: Scope of Processing
scope.description: The DPA covers all personal data processed through the HR platform, including employee records, recruitment data, payroll information, and HR documents. Processing is limited to the purposes specified in the service contract.

duration.title: Duration and Termination
duration.description: The DPA is in effect for the duration of the service contract. Upon termination, we will return or securely delete all personal data within 30 days, unless retention is required by applicable law (e.g. statutory accounting retention periods).

obligations.title: Processor Obligations
obligations.description: We process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, ensure all staff with access are bound by confidentiality, implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures, assist with data subject requests, and support compliance assessments.

subProcessors.title: 3. Sub-processors
subProcessors.description: We use the following sub-processors to deliver our services. Each sub-processor is bound by a data processing agreement and has been assessed for GDPR compliance:

security.title: 4. Technical and Organisational Measures
security.description: We implement comprehensive security measures as required by Article 32 of the GDPR to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems:

measures.title: Security Measures (Article 32 GDPR)
measures.encryption: Encryption: TLS 1.3 for data in transit, AES-256 for data at rest within our EU infrastructure
measures.access: Access Control: Role-based access control (RBAC), multi-factor authentication (MFA), and the principle of least privilege
measures.audits: Audit Logging: Comprehensive logging of all data accesses and modifications with tamper-resistant log storage
measures.training: Staff Training: Annual GDPR and data protection training for all employees, with specialist training for the development team
measures.backup: Business Continuity: Automated encrypted backups with point-in-time recovery, stored in geographically separated EU data centres
measures.monitoring: Monitoring: 24/7 security monitoring with automated threat detection and incident response procedures

rights.title: 5. Data Subject Rights
rights.description: Under Chapter III of the GDPR, data subjects have the following rights. We facilitate the exercise of all these rights through our platform and DPO:

inform.title: Right to Be Informed (Articles 13–14)
inform.description: You have the right to clear, transparent information about how your personal data is collected and used. This GDPR page and our Privacy Policy fulfil this obligation.

access.title: Right of Access (Article 15)
access.description: You may request a complete copy of all personal data we hold about you, including processing purposes, data categories, recipients, and retention periods.

rectification.title: Right to Rectification (Article 16)
rectification.description: You may request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. Most data can be updated directly through account settings.

erasure.title: Right to Erasure (Article 17)
erasure.description: You may request deletion of your personal data when processing is no longer necessary, you withdraw consent, or processing is unlawful — subject to statutory retention requirements under applicable law.

restrict.title: Right to Restriction (Article 18)
restrict.description: You may request restriction of processing while data accuracy is being verified, the lawfulness of processing is being contested, or you require the data for legal claims.

portability.title: Right to Data Portability (Article 20)
portability.description: You may receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format (JSON or CSV) and transmit it to another controller.

objection.title: Right to Object (Article 21)
objection.description: You may object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes at any time. We will cease processing unless we can demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds.

automated.title: Automated Decision-Making (Article 22)
automated.description: You have the right not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects. Our AI features (e.g. candidate matching) provide recommendations only — all final decisions are always made by humans.

exercise.title: How to Exercise Your Rights
exercise.description: We have established a streamlined process for handling data subject requests:

process.title: Request Process
process.step1: Submit your request to dpo@hr.com with your full name and account email
process.step2: We will verify your identity within 3 business days
process.step3: Your request will be processed within 30 calendar days (Article 12(3) GDPR)
process.step4: You will receive confirmation and any requested data in your chosen format

timeline.title: Response Timeline
timeline.content: We respond within 30 days. For complex or numerous requests, this period may be extended by 60 days with prior notice, as permitted by Article 12(3) GDPR.

transfers.title: 6. Cross-border Transfers
transfers.description: HR stores all primary data within the European Union (AWS Frankfurt, eu-central-1). Where a transfer of data outside the EU/EEA is necessary for specific services, we rely on the following GDPR-approved transfer mechanisms:

safeguards.title: Transfer Safeguards
safeguards.adequacy: EU Adequacy Decisions (Article 45 GDPR) — for transfers to countries with equivalent protection
safeguards.sccs: Standard Contractual Clauses (Article 46(2)(c) GDPR) — approved by European Commission Decision 2021/914, with supplementary measures per EDPB Recommendations 01/2020
safeguards.bcrs: Binding Corporate Rules (Article 47 GDPR) — where applicable for multinational sub-processors
safeguards.derogations: Derogations under Article 49 — only as a last resort, with explicit consent and individual risk assessment

breaches.title: 8. Breach Notification
breaches.description: We maintain a comprehensive data breach response plan in accordance with Articles 33–34 of the GDPR:

notification.title: Notification Obligations
notification.content: In the event of a personal data breach that is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals, we will: (1) notify the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach (Article 33); (2) notify affected data subjects without undue delay where the breach is likely to result in a high risk (Article 34); (3) document all breaches in our internal breach register, regardless of risk level.

compliance.title: 9. Compliance Documentation
compliance.description: We maintain the following documentation to demonstrate GDPR compliance (Article 5(2) accountability principle):

items.ropa: Records of Processing Activities (RoPA), as required by Article 30
items.dpia: Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) for high-risk processing activities (Article 35)
items.policies: Internal data protection policies and procedures, reviewed annually
items.training: Staff training records and annual compliance certificates
items.incidents: Data breach register and incident response documentation

cookies.title: Cookies and Tracking
cookies.description: The use of cookies and similar tracking technologies is governed by our Cookie Policy.
cookies.moreInfo: For full details, please see our

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.description: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:
controller.addressDetails: Business Park Sofia, Building 2, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria

dataTypes.title: Types of Data We Process
dataTypes.description: We process various categories of personal data depending on your relationship with us:

personal.title: Personal Identification Data
personal.description: Basic personal information provided during registration and use:

sensitive.title: Special Category Data (Article 9)
sensitive.description: Processed only with explicit consent or where necessary for employment law obligations:

items.health: Health data (e.g. sick leave) — processed under Art. 9(2)(b) for employment obligations
items.disability: Disability information — processed for reasonable adjustment requirements under applicable law
items.ethnicity: Ethnic origin — only where voluntarily provided for diversity monitoring
items.religion: Religious beliefs — only for managing holiday preferences where voluntarily provided

technical.title: Technical Data
technical.description: Automatically collected when using the platform:

items.location: Approximate geographic location derived from IP

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.description: We process personal data on the legal bases established in Article 6 of the GDPR:

consent.title: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
consent.description: For marketing emails, non-essential cookies, and voluntary data collection.

contract.title: Contractual Necessity (Art. 6(1)(b))
contract.description: For service delivery, account management, billing, and customer support.

legal.title: Legal Obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))

summary.title: Our GDPR Commitment
summary.content: HR Ltd is committed to full compliance with the GDPR and applicable data protection legislation. We implement technical and organisational measures that exceed the minimum requirements, conduct regular compliance audits, and maintain transparent communication with our users about data processing activities.
summary.commitment: Protecting your data is our priority.
summary.promise: We continuously review and update our practices to ensure the highest standard of data protection.
